title: olearia lasiophylla
intro: Olearia lasiophylla, also known as the New Zealand tree daisy or frost flower, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Olearia lasiophylla include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often made from the leaves and flowers.
